item.title: AdaPLD：一种无需训练的高效文本生成加速方法
item.content:encoded:
AdaPLD通过自适应优化检索与草稿生成，提升文本生成速度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为AdaPLD的无需训练的文本生成加速方法。该方法通过自适应优化检索和草稿生成，解决了现有基于重用的方法在表面形式变化下的召回率低以及确定性片段复制脆弱的问题。AdaPLD在保持高精度词汇重用的同时，利用语义相似性在词汇匹配失败时恢复额外的重用机会，并构建分支重用假设以应对续写不确定性。实验表明，AdaPLD在多个基准测试中显著减少了目标模型前向传递次数，实现了最高3.10倍的解码加速。

item.title: 基于p-进数与拓扑数据分析的基因组序列分类框架pVR
item.content:encoded:
pVR利用p-进数和拓扑分析实现无对齐基因组分类，性能优于多个基准。



摘要： 本文提出pVR，一种结合p-进数和拓扑数据分析的无对齐基因组序列分类框架。通过p-进数距离和k-mer频率L1距离构建双过滤维多利亚-里普斯复形，生成拓扑特征用于机器学习分类。理论分析表明其对度量扰动稳定且对素数选择不变，且单p-进轴信息不足，需双过滤才能获得有效同调。在十二个基因组基准测试中，pVR在低样本数据集上优于四个现有基线，最高提升21个百分点，仅在SARS-CoV-2变异数据集表现不佳。同时，pVR在三个低样本基准上优于Nucleotide Transformer v2的零样本嵌入。代码已公开。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的传染病爆发期间个体决策模拟研究
item.content:encoded:
研究利用大语言模型模拟个体在流感样疾病中的行为决策。



摘要： 本文提出一个结合大语言模型和空间分布的代理模拟框架，用于研究传染病爆发期间个体的行为决策。通过将真实人口普查数据与LLM生成的决策相结合，模拟了旧金山和亚特兰大地区的自我报告行为。研究比较了三种决策场景：独立推理、家庭影响和信息框架，并发现收入和教育水平是报告率差异的主要驱动因素，地理、模型选择和信息框架也有一定影响。该框架能够生成反映社会和地理异质性的合成数据，支持空间流行病学建模和行为分析。

item.title: 主动探索提升成人对合取因果规则的推理能力
item.content:encoded:
主动探索改善成人对合取因果规则的理解，但仍需更多测试。



摘要： 研究表明，成人通常难以识别需要多个原因同时存在的合取因果规则，但在主动探索情境下，这种能力有所提升。研究采用改良的‘blicket detector’任务，让参与者自由干预以识别因果对象。结果显示，尽管合取规则仍需更多测试才能推断，但主动探索显著提高了推理效果。此外，研究还比较了人类与大型语言模型的表现，发现部分模型在假设推断准确性上接近人类，但在探索策略效率和合取-析取表现差异上仍有不足。

item.title: 大型语言模型的安全对齐引发新漏洞
item.content:encoded:
安全对齐导致模型易受后验攻击，揭示安全机制潜在缺陷。



摘要： 本文研究发现，大型语言模型（LLMs）在安全对齐过程中虽然能够识别和拒绝有害请求，但这种能力反而引入了新的安全漏洞——后验攻击。通过单次查询即可绕过防护机制，使模型生成原本会被内部分类器标记为不安全的内容。实验覆盖30个开源模型及前沿模型，结果显示安全性越强的模型越容易被攻击。作者提出“安全悖论”，证明安全对齐的提升会增强模型的后验脆弱性，并通过强化学习验证了安全判断能力与攻击敏感度之间的因果关系。研究指出当前对齐方法可能存在结构性缺陷，需进一步优化防御机制。

item.title: 医学图像分割中的数据集驱动设计框架研究
item.content:encoded:
医学图像分割需依据数据集特性设计模型，MS-DKC框架提升分割设计的可追溯性。



摘要： 本文提出医学分割数据集知识卡（MS-DKC）框架，用于明确数据集对模型的要求，包括前景占据、形态学、边界模糊性等因素。通过图像、标注质量、部署风险等描述符，MS-DKC将这些因素映射到失败模式和设计先验，使分割设计更透明。在DRIVE、ISIC2018和ACDC数据集上的实验表明，不同数据集需要不同的模型设计和评估指标，支持基于数据集条件的设计方法。

item.title: 基于批判机制的多智能体框架提升数学推理可靠性
item.content:encoded:
通过多智能体与批判机制提升数学推理准确性与可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于批判机制的异构多智能体框架，以提高大型语言模型在复杂数学推理任务中的可靠性。该框架包含多个具有不同专业能力的LLM代理，并通过一个由批评驱动的自适应学习系统对推理过程进行评估和指导。系统采用生成-验证结构，验证器不仅判断答案正确性，还提供反馈以引导重新生成解决方案，从而实现动态错误修正并防止错误累积。实验表明，该方法在GSM8K基准测试中比单次推理和非批判模型提升了13%的准确率。研究还发现，异构性和批判机制可减少对大模型的依赖，使小型模型也能达到相近性能。消融实验进一步证明性能提升主要来源于批判反馈循环，而非模型规模。

item.title: Tangram：优化非均匀KV缓存的LLM推理系统

Tangram提升LLM推理效率，解决KV缓存不均问题。

item.title: 基于对抗游戏的机器人安全策略危险感知学习框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种对抗式游戏框架用于机器人安全策略的危险感知学习。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于对抗游戏的框架，通过合成场景实现机器人安全策略的危险感知学习。该框架将场景生成建模为红队与蓝队之间的对抗游戏，红队负责构建危险情境以探索潜在故障，蓝队则逐步优化安全策略以防止这些故障。这种迭代过程能够高效发现高风险边缘案例，弥补传统随机模拟或人工枚举的不足。结合经典风险建模、对抗场景生成和现代学习范式，该研究为复杂现实环境中物理AI系统的安全性嵌入提供了一种可扩展的路径。论文描述了当前的研究工作，贡献包括问题定义和解决方案架构。

item.title: 语言模型是否表现出人类的零忽略效应
item.content:encoded:
研究语言模型是否具有人类的零忽略认知偏差。



摘要： 本文探讨了大语言模型（LLMs）在处理语言时是否表现出人类的认知偏差——零忽略效应。该效应指人类倾向于忽略那些使命题为真但基于空集的零模型。研究通过结构启动范式，分析LLMs在涉及和不涉及零忽略效应的推理任务中的表现。实验结果显示，所研究的LLMs可能并未表现出零忽略效应。研究结果有助于理解LLMs与人类在语言处理上的异同。

item.title: GLASS：一种零样本自回归文本转语音的可组合声学风格控制框架
item.content:encoded:
GLASS通过后生成奖励学习实现零样本TTS的声学风格控制。



摘要： 本文提出GLASS，一个用于零样本自回归文本转语音（TTS）的可组合声学风格控制框架。与传统依赖风格标签的方法不同，GLASS通过后生成奖励来学习控制策略。在零样本TTS中，说话人提示通常将说话人身份与语调等属性混合，难以单独调整风格而不改变提示。GLASS将每个声学属性视为基于奖励的控制方向，通过冻结TTS主干并训练轻量级LoRA适配器，使用语音标记长度和平均F0作为风格奖励，WER作为可懂性锚点。由于每个控制以LoRA权重更新形式表示，独立训练的适配器可通过线性LoRA算术进行交换、插值和组合，无需重新训练主干。实验表明，GLASS在保持自然度、说话人相似性和可懂性的前提下实现了目标风格变化，并展示了多轴组合的平滑性。

item.title: 面向机构文档的数据快照提取基准与评估框架
item.content:encoded:
提出数据快照提取任务，提升机构文档中分析性图表的识别能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了用于识别和定位机构文档中语义有意义的视觉内容（如图表）的基准数据集和评估框架。该数据集涵盖人道主义报告、世界银行政策研究工作文件和项目评估文档，并包含可重用分析信息的图表标注。研究发现，现有模型在通用学术基准上表现良好，但在操作性机构文档上的泛化能力不足，主要问题包括分析性内容与非分析性内容的混淆、复合分析对象的碎片化以及上下文信息的不完整提取。研究结果突显了通用文档布局分析与实际操作有用的数据快照提取之间的差距，并提供了源PDF、标注数据集、元数据和源代码以支持未来的研究。

item.title: 基于上下文的代理安全监控研究
item.content:encoded:
研究代理在游戏环境中奖励黑客行为的安全监控方法。



摘要： 本文研究了在Gameable ALFWorld和WebShop环境中，ReAct风格代理的奖励黑客行为监测。通过引入基于激活的奖励黑客评分、令牌级熵值和决策上下文特征，发现基于School-of-Reward-Hacks数据集微调的适配器能够将奖励黑客倾向转化为代理行为选择，尤其是在环境提供代理奖励机会时。然而，仅依赖激活动态无法有效缓解此类行为。高奖励黑客激活标识了潜在策略状态，但不必然导致立即的滥用行为。通过熵值和上下文校准的内部特征，可以提升风险评估效果，而激活方向调整则进一步减少了混合适配器模式下的代理滥用行为。整体研究表明，上下文校准的内部监控对代理安全至关重要。

item.title: 基于前向后向扩散框架的时序数据生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种适应性时序数据生成框架，提升时间依赖建模效果。



摘要： 本文针对时序数据生成中的时间依赖性和信息结构建模难题，提出一种基于前向后向扩散的适应性框架。该方法通过逐步注入和去除噪声，并基于已生成的历史信息进行条件建模，确保生成过程的适应性。研究引入了新的分数匹配目标以实现高效并行训练，在通用框架下建立了严格的统计保证，并通过ReLU网络验证了分数近似、分数估计和分布估计的结果。实验表明，该方法在合成数据（如ARMA模型和高斯过程）以及均值-方差最优投资组合构建中表现优异。

item.title: 基于轨迹采样的通量匹配方法用于反应路径分析
item.content:encoded:
通量匹配框架从反应轨迹中学习反应路径和坐标。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为通量匹配（Flux Matching）的框架，该框架直接从反应轨迹数据中学习两个互补对象：电流速度 $u(z)$ 和标量势 $h(z)$。$u(z)$ 的流线可追踪主要反应路径，而 $h(z)$ 通过加权赫尔姆霍茨-霍奇分解获得，作为数据驱动的反应坐标。两者在反应路径集合上最小化二次泛函，类似于生成模型中的流匹配损失，且无需了解底层动力学或稳态分布。与基于通量的方法不同，$u$ 和 $h$ 在投影到非马尔可夫集体变量时仍保持定义清晰，其等值面可作为自适应界面以提升采样效率。该方法在分子系统中验证了电流速度轨迹生成和速率常数计算的有效性。

item.title: QCFuse：提升RAG缓存融合效率的查询感知选择器
item.content:encoded:
QCFuse提升RAG缓存融合效率，实现高质量生成。



摘要： 本文提出QCFuse，一种用于RAG缓存融合的压缩视图查询感知选择器。通过块锚点查询探测和关键层分析，QCFuse在不牺牲生成质量的前提下，显著提升了预填充阶段的处理速度。实验表明，在保持全预填充质量的情况下，QCFuse相比完整预填充和ProphetKV基线分别提升了1.7倍和1.5倍的预填充速度。该方法有效解决了现有选择器在质量和效率之间的权衡问题。

item.title: CogManip：评估大语言模型隐性心理操控风险的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
CogManip评估15种隐性操控策略，揭示LLM安全风险。



摘要： 本文介绍了CogManip，一个用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）在复杂人机交互中潜在隐性心理操控风险的全面基准测试。该基准覆盖1,000个多轮对话场景，并由人类专家验证。对13个代表性模型的系统评估发现，不同模型在操控风险上存在显著差异，尤其DeepSeek-V3.2对系统提示高度敏感，凸显了基于提示的防御工程和隐含目标审计的重要性。CogManip为评估现代LLMs的隐性心理影响和动态策略选择提供了有力工具。

item.title: MARDoc：基于结构化记忆的多模态长文档问答框架
item.content:encoded:
MARDoc通过结构化记忆提升多模态长文档问答效果。



摘要： 本文提出MARDoc，一个基于记忆感知优化的代理框架，用于多模态长文档问答任务。该框架将问答过程拆分为三个专门代理：探索者负责多粒度多模态检索，精炼者将交互痕迹提炼为结构化证据和推理记忆，反思者检查证据充分性并提供反馈。MARDoc使用动态更新的结构化记忆代替完整交互历史，减少上下文噪声，同时保留关键事实及其逻辑关系。实验表明，MARDoc在MMLongBench-Doc和DocBench数据集上表现优异，验证了结构化记忆在代理文档问答中的有效性。

item.title: Inverse Design of Realizable Metasurface based Absorbers using Improved Conditioning and Diversity Enhanced Progressively Growing GANs
item.content:encoded: Metasurfaces enable precise manipulation of electromagnetic waves for applications such as beam steering, sensing, and stealth technology. However, inverse design of metasurfaces with targeted EM responses remains challenging due to the computational expense of iterative full wave simulation driven optimization and the limited conditioning fidelity and diversity of existing generative approaches. To address these challenges, this paper presents a generative inverse design framework for controllable and physically consistent metasurface synthesis under continuous spectral constraints. The proposed approach employs a progressively growing Wasserstein generative adversarial network with gradient penalty integrated with feature wise linear modulation based conditioning for stable propagation of continuous spectral and fabrication constraints. EM consistency is embedded directly into the generative learning process through a surrogate assisted spectral alignment loss, enabling physics constrained generation during training. Further, a determinantal point process based diversity regularization strategy is incorporated to generate geometrically diverse yet spectrally consistent realizations for the same target response. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is demonstrated through the generation of practically realizable metasurface absorbers exhibiting diverse reflection characteristics in the frequency range of 2 to 18 GHz. EM simulations validate that the generated designs meet the target specifications with high accuracy. The final proposed framework achieved an average mean squared error of 0.0052, diversity score of 0.8730, band alignment accuracy of 0.8533, and a valid EM design generation percentage of 89.57, clearly demonstrating its capability to generate highly accurate, diverse, electromagnetically consistent and fabrication realizable metasurface configurations.

item.title: Representation Learning Enables Scalable Multitask Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Scaling reinforcement learning (RL) to diverse multitask settings remains a central challenge. While recent advances in model-based RL achieve strong performance, they rely on planning and complex training pipelines, making it unclear which components are essential for scalability. We revisit this question and argue that the primary driver of scalable multitask RL is not model-based control, but \emph{representation learning}. In particular, we show that combining predictive, model-based representations with high-capacity value function approximation is sufficient to achieve strong performance, even without planning. We evaluate a simple model-free algorithm, MR.Q, coupled with auxiliary predictive objectives into a scalable actor-critic architecture. This approach outperforms a recent world-model-based method and a range of deep RL baselines across a diverse suite of multitask continuous control tasks, while significantly reducing computational overhead and improving wall-clock efficiency. We observe consistent improvements with increased model capacity and show through ablations that predictive representation learning is critical for performance.

item.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
item.content:encoded: Large language models often improve reasoning by generating explicit chain-of-thought (CoT), demonstrating the importance of intermediate computation. However, textual CoT forces this computation through a discrete, serial, and communication-oriented token stream: each reasoning step must be verbalized before the model can proceed, even when the underlying update is semantic, uncertain, or only partially formed. Latent reasoning offers a higher-bandwidth alternative by performing intermediate computation in compact continuous states before committing to text. Yet existing latent-reasoning methods often sacrifice key advantages that make CoT effective in autoregressive language models, including native left-to-right generation, probabilistic sampling, compatibility with KV-cache decoding, and tractable likelihood estimation. We propose NF-CoT, a latent reasoning framework that preserves these advantages by modeling continuous thoughts with normalizing flows. NF-CoT instantiates a TARFlow-style normalizing flow inside the LLM backbone, defining a tractable probability model over compact continuous thoughts distilled from explicit CoT. Continuous-thought positions are generated by an NF head, while text positions are generated by the standard LM head within the same causal stream. This design provides exact likelihoods for latent thoughts, enables probabilistic left-to-right decoding with the original KV cache, and supports direct policy-gradient optimization in the latent reasoning space. On code-generation benchmarks, NF-CoT improves pass rates over explicit-CoT and prior latent-reasoning baselines while substantially reducing intermediate-reasoning cost.

item.title: Performance Evaluation of GraphCast for Medium-Range Weather Forecasting over Brazil
item.content:encoded: The paradigm of global weather forecasting is rapidly shifting with the emergence of Machine Learning Weather Prediction models (MLWP). While these data-driven architectures demonstrate remarkable global skill, regional benchmarks in the Global South remain scarce, leaving their efficacy in complex, highly convective environments largely unverified. This study evaluates the performance of GraphCast operational against the deterministic ECMWF IFS HRES as baseline across four distinct Brazilian climatic sub-regions. Utilizing a scalable, cloud-native pipeline and the WeatherBench-X framework for benchmarking weather models, we assess selected tropospheric variables ($T_{850}$, $Q_{850}$, $Z_{500}$) over four selected seasonal windows, employing the operational IFS analysis as the ground truth to calculate the statistical metrics for both models. Results reveal a regime-dependent skill profile. During the austral winter, GraphCast underperforms in the medium range (lead days 2-7) for $Z_{500}$ when resolving fast-propagating baroclinic systems over southern Brazil, but regains an advantage in the extended range, where its inherent smoothing of chaotic small-scale variability becomes beneficial under deterministic skill metrics. Conversely, during the austral summer wet season, GraphCast accurately captures large-scale moisture transport while intrinsically dampening the high-frequency convective variability that degrades deterministic NWP temperature forecasts. These findings establish a baseline for Brazil and define the specific physical boundaries that will guide future ``tropicalization'' efforts, aiming to optimize these foundational AI models for regional resilience.

item.title: Learning Visual Spatial Planning from Symbolic State via Modality-Gap-Aware Self-Distillation
item.content:encoded: While vision-language models excel at general multimodal understanding, they still struggle with visual spatial planning. We attribute this to a perception-reasoning modality gap: visual planning requires models to infer latent state structures from pixels and then reason over the recovered structure to produce valid actions, whereas symbolic planning directly leverages explicit objects and constraints. This creates dual bottlenecks in visual state recovery and multi-step planning. To address this, we propose MGSD, a two-stage modality-gap-aware self-distillation framework. First, a cold-start grounding stage equips the visual student with reliable state representations, minimizing early perception noise. Second, a privileged teacher transfers planning capabilities via on-policy distillation, using explicit symbolic states to supervise the student's own visual rollout prefixes. Crucially, symbolic data is used strictly during training, leaving inference purely visual. Experiments on visual planning benchmarks show that MGSD consistently improves visual planning across both 4B and 8B backbones, raising the macro average by 19.3% and 18.4%, respectively. The resulting models narrow the gap to symbolic-input upper bounds, while ablations and diagnostics confirm that the improvement comes from both visual state recovery and optimal-path reasoning. These results suggest that modality-gap-aware self-distillation improves not only how models perceive actionable states, but also how they plan over the inferred structure. Code is available at https://github.com/Oranger-l/MGSD.

item.title: Comparison of Deep Learning Frameworks For Rice Disease Mapping From UAV Multispectral Imaging
item.content:encoded: In this study, UAV multispectral imagery is used to segment the severity of bacterial leaf blight (BLB) in rice using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer-based models. The evaluated architectures include U-Net with a ResNet- 101 encoder, U-Net++ with EfficientNet-B3 and EfficientNetB7, DeepLabV3+, and SegFormer, all trained under a common pipeline with three input configurations (multispectral only, multispectral+NDVI, and multispectral+NDRE). Experiments are conducted using the publicly available BLB dataset with performance reported using mean IoU (mIoU), mean F1 (mF1), mean accuracy (mAcc), precision, and recall. U-Net++ with EfficientNet-B3 achieved the highest performance, with an mIoU of 97.62%. SegFormer obtained lower segmentation accuracy but comparable inference speed. Overall, the results indicate that lightweight CNN backbones remain more reliable for operational BLB monitoring while integration of vegetation indices provides small and consistent improvements. The study also highlights the value of standardised UAV datasets to compare disease mapping methods and encourages the use of CNN architectures for field implementation.

item.title: Fast and Robust Convergence Rate for TD(0) with Linear Function Approximation, Universal Learning Steps and I.I.D. Samples
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we study the finite-time behavior of the TD(0) temporal-difference method with linear function approximation (LFA). We consider on-policy independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) samples, a constant learning step, and the Polyak-Juditsky averaging method. We establish a new convergence rate, for the Mean-Square Error (MSE) on the approximated function, that is (i) fast in the sense that it admits an optimal dependency in the number of iterations k (i.e., of order 1/k), (ii) robust to ill-conditioning: it only depends on an initial error and modelindependent constants and (iii) sharp up to a multiplicative constant lower than 11. In particular, it does not depend on the smallest eigenvalue of the uncentered covariance matrix of the linear parametrization, unlike all pre-existing O(1/k) rates in the TD(0) literature. We also introduce PCTD(0), a variant of TD(0), which benefits from better convergence properties under an additional assumption of strong mixing on the Markov Chain.

item.title: Data Flow Control: Data Safety Policies for AI Agents
item.content:encoded: Agents increasingly generate SQL, orchestrate pipelines, and automate data analysis on behalf of users. While recent work improves query correctness, correctness is not safety. A query may be semantically valid yet violate regulatory, privacy, or business constraints that govern how data may be combined and released. We argue that enforcing such constraints is fundamentally a data infrastructure problem. This paper introduces Data Flow Control (DFC), a framework to declaratively specify and guarantee policy enforcement over tuple-level data flows within a DBMS query. A key challenge is defining a policy language that is optimizer-invariant yet efficient to enforce at scale. We formalize data safety as aggregate predicates over provenance monomials and present Passant, a portable query rewriting layer that enforces DFC policies without materializing provenance. Across five DBMS engines -- DuckDB, Umbra, PostgreSQL, DataFusion, and SQLServer -- Passant achieves ~0% overhead and outperforms alternatives by orders of magnitude. As a result, Data Flow Control is the first step towards moving data safety from prompts and post-hoc checks into the data infrastructure. Data Flow Control is available open source at https://github.com/dataflowcontrol/data-flow-control.

item.title: Can LLMs Be Constrained to the Past? Improving Knowledge Cutoff through Recall-Based Prompting
item.content:encoded: Prompted knowledge cutoff instructs a large language model (LLM) to act as if information beyond a specified cutoff date were unavailable. However, prior work mainly relies on direct-answer generation, which struggles when post-cutoff knowledge is not explicitly queried but is only causally related to the question. To address this limitation, we propose two recall-based prompting strategies: Self-Recall (SR), which asks the model to restate its cutoff constraint, and Question-Recall (QR), which requires the model to recall question-relevant information valid under the cutoff. Across three existing benchmarks, our methods outperform both direct-answer prompting and conventional step-by-step reasoning baselines, with particularly strong improvements on counterfactual questions. To investigate robustness across different cutoff settings, we further construct the Multi-cutoff Historical Event Benchmark (MHEB), which evaluates the same question under multiple cutoff years. Results show that knowledge cutoff performance varies with cutoff distance, while combining SR and QR consistently yields the best performance.

item.title: ShotCrop$^3$: Cropping Human-Centric Images into Cinematic Triple-Shot Compositions
item.content:encoded: Prior work on aesthetic composition typically produces a single aesthetically pleasing crop, overlooking the narrative value of composing multiple shots from one scene. In practice, multi-shot composition is critical for downstream creative workflows: commercial posters often require multiple crops with different emphases (e.g., context, subject, and emotion/product details) to present key story beats. Therefore, we propose \textbf{Triple-Shot Compositions (TSC)}, a composition task that generates a three-shot set -- establishing, medium, and close-up -- from a single human-centric image, each paired with a brief shot description to support visual narration. To learn TSC with limited expert annotations, we introduce \textbf{ShotCrop} which undergoes a three-stage training process: it first applies Chain-of-Thought supervised fine-tuning to establish basic reasoning and aesthetic shot-cropping skills, then performs semi-supervised fine-tuning with high-confidence pseudo labels to further enhance aesthetic capability, and is finally optimized with Group Relative Policy Optimization for \textbf{ShotCrop} (GRPO-S) using a composite reward tailored for it. Specifically, our pseudo-labeling strategy combines MLLM-based scoring, aesthetic assessment, and CLIP similarity to retain high-confidence training signals. In addition, we present TSC-Bench, a benchmark of 1.2k expert-annotated test cases. Notably, ShotCrop achieves an average improvement of \textbf{2.82} times over GPT-5 in shot localization accuracy.

item.title: Proper Scoring Rules for Right-Censored Survival Data
item.content:encoded: Proper scoring rules provide a rigorous theoretical basis for the training and evaluation of probabilistic forecasts. However, in the presence of right censoring, the event time is only partially observed, rendering conventional scoring rules inapplicable in their standard form. We propose a framework for proper scoring of right-censored survival outcomes based on a simple idea: first, map the predictive distribution through the censoring mechanism, then apply the underlying proper score on the induced observed-data law. This yields localized scores for fixed censoring times and marginalized scores when the censoring time is random or only partially observed. The resulting construction recovers familiar right-censored likelihood and IPCW-type criteria within a coherent framework, while also yielding right-censored versions of the CRPS, pinball loss, Brier score, and energy score. We show that the marginalized score is proper under conditional independent censoring and strictly proper on the identifiable region. The same principle also leads to censored engression, a sample-based learning objective for multivariate right-censored survival modeling. In experiments, our scores correctly rank the oracle forecast across several censoring regimes, whereas forecast-dependent plug-in weighted scores can exhibit ranking reversals. Censored engression likewise substantially improves over naive training on censored outcomes.

item.title: Seeing Time: Benchmarking Chronological Reasoning and Shortcut Biases in Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Recent advancements in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have significantly enhanced their ability to interpret complex visual semantics, yet their capacity for chronological reasoning remains under-explored. In this paper, we introduce a novel benchmark specifically designed to evaluate how VLMs perceive and reason about chronological information within and across images. Unlike existing video-based benchmarks that focus on frame sequencing, our work delves into the underlying logic of chronological judgment and the expansion toward multimodal integration. To facilitate this, we construct three specialized datasets: one containing visually similar objects spanning long historical durations, another categorized by diverse event and object types, and a third pairing images with time-sensitive news text for cross-modal alignment. Through extensive experiments, we analyze whether models exhibit performance disparities across categories and, crucially, explore whether they rely on ``incorrect shortcuts'', such as image color rather than genuine chronological features. Our results reveal that while VLMs show promise, they frequently exploit superficial cues like grayscale versus color filters to bypass authentic chronological reasoning. By providing these high-quality datasets and a rigorous evaluation framework, we offer a diagnostic tool to identify current limitations and guide the development of more robust, logically grounded multimodal models. The source code is shown in https://github.com/LuoRenqiang/ChronoVision.

item.title: Less is MoE: Trimming Experts in Domain-Specialist Language Models
item.content:encoded: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models achieve strong performance through conditional computation, but their large parameter footprint poses deployment challenges. Prior MoE compression approaches catastrophically fail when evaluated on general-purpose benchmarks beyond commonsense reasoning. We trace this failure to the granularity of compression: important capabilities are distributed across experts but concentrated in FFN sparse intermediate dimensions. To identify these dimensions, we use Fisher importance which outperforms activation-, router-score-, and magnitude-based alternatives, and identifies tiny sets of task-critical dimensions: in Qwen1.5-MoE, removing as few as 12 of 1.35M routed-FFN intermediate dimensions collapses GSM8K accuracy while largely preserving factual-knowledge performance. Building on this, we propose Fisher-MoE, which operates within FFN to remove intermediate dimensions ranked by Fisher importance. At the same 50% MoE compression ratio, Fisher-MoE preserves model capability, while reducing weight memory by ~45% and improving inference throughput by 21%. These findings suggest intermediate dimension granularity is an effective unit for both compression and ranking where capability concentrates in MoE models.

item.title: Ouvia: A User-centered Framework for Measuring Usability of Speech Translation in Real-World Communication Scenarios
item.content:encoded: Speech translation (ST) is increasingly adopted in user applications, yet its evaluation largely focuses on decontextualized testbeds and holistic quality, rather than end users' communication needs. We introduce Ouvia, an evaluation framework for measuring user-perceived usability of speech translation outputs in real-world settings. Ouvia focuses on one-to-one communication: an English speaker needs to convey a request to a Portuguese speaker, and the message is automatically translated. Through a custom web app and multi-phase study design, we collect more than 1,750 such interactions in healthcare and everyday situations, mediated by four ST systems, involving speakers from three English dialects and two genders. We find that modern ST serves people only to a limited extent -- only around half of interactions are rated as usable -- with significant gaps in reported usability across demographic groups. Moreover, among quality metrics, we find that QA-based evaluation is a substantially stronger predictor of real-world usability than standard approaches. Together, these findings stress the importance of situated, user-centered evaluation frameworks that go beyond holistic quality scores and attend to who the technology serves -- and how well.

item.title: Let It Be Simple: One-Step Action Generation for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.content:encoded: Diffusion-based vision-language-action (VLA) models often inherit the image-generation view: actions are generated by iterative denoising. We argue that VLA action generation has a different condition-target structure: the policy is conditioned on rich observations, language, and state, but predicts only a compact, low-dimensional action chunk. Under this asymmetry, strong one-step action generation should not necessarily require the advanced one-step methods developed for image synthesis. We keep standard velocity prediction and add no teacher model, distillation stage, or auxiliary objective; in our main recipe, we simply bias the training time distribution toward high-noise states. We first isolate the effect in a controlled MNIST grid-to-sequence task, then test it with extensive robot-policy experiments. Across standard LIBERO, LIBERO-Plus, and LIBERO-Pro, one-step policies trained with high-noise biased schedules generally match ten-step decoding under the same recipe, and on standard LIBERO can exceed ten-step policies trained with a uniform time distribution. A real-robot bimanual YAM RSS evaluation gives a small-sample cross-architecture check of the same sampler trend. On a 1.4B VLM model with a 30M action head, one-step decoding reaches 95.6\% on LIBERO-Long. These results show that strong one-step VLA action generation can emerge from standard diffusion training, without importing the full few-step diffusion machinery developed for image generation.

item.title: Entropy-Based Evaluation of AI Agents: A Lightweight Framework for Measuring Behavioral Patterns
item.content:encoded: AI agents are commonly evaluated using task success, reward, latency, and cost. These metrics are useful, but they often miss important aspects of agent behavior: whether an agent explores too much, repeats itself too rigidly, uses tools effectively, reduces uncertainty over time, or remains robust across repeated runs. This paper proposes Entropy-Based Evaluation of AI Agents (EEA), a lightweight framework for measuring agent behavior through entropy. Rather than treating intelligence as only final task completion, EEA studies the structure of the agents decision process. The framework introduces action entropy, trajectory entropy, tool entropy, information gain, exploration efficiency, and robustness entropy. These metrics are intended to complement, not replace, traditional evaluation methods. We also present a practical Python implementation designed to integrate with agent frameworks such as LangChain, Google ADK, custom agent loops, and stored observability traces.

item.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
item.content:encoded: Robot manipulation alternates between low-risk transit phases that call for fast execution and high-risk contact stages that demand slow, precise motion. Yet existing Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) only inherit a single fixed speed from training demonstrations. Prior efforts to accelerate VLAs through model compression, KV-cache reuse, or reinforcement learning only shift the policy from one fixed speed to another, and leave deceleration almost unexplored. We observe that the magnitude of each predicted action already governs how fast the robot moves, opening a direct route to controllable execution speed. We turn this observation into TempoVLA, a single VLA whose execution speed is controlled by an explicit condition. TempoVLA combines two coupled components. (1) A data-side Variable-Speed Trajectory Augmentation (VSTA) that re-times demonstration to any target speed by merging or splitting actions while preserving its motion semantics. (2) A model-side conditioning mechanism that feeds the speed to the policy. Statistics show that VSTA reaches the requested speed with negligible motion error. Experiments in simulation and on real-world tasks demonstrate that TempoVLA achieves flexible speed control in both directions, while VSTA additionally boosts the default $1\times$ performance via better data utilization. Furthermore, by cooperating with a large multimodal model, TempoVLA realizes dynamic speed control, accelerating through low-risk phases and decelerating for high-risk ones.

item.title: Monte Carlo Steklov Operators for Large-Scale Geometry Processing in the Wild
item.content:encoded: Intrinsic methods fill the default toolbox for geometry processing on meshes. Intrinsic operators, in particular the Laplacian, underlie methods that require invariance to isometry and have hence been employed in many algorithms for shape analysis, learning, and editing. However, intrinsic methods are predicated on assumptions that quickly become brittle when working with in-the-wild geometry, where (i) mesh quality is not guaranteed, and (ii) many meshes are modeled with multiple connected components. In such settings, volumetric constructions are better-defined, since restrictions on surface topology can be relaxed. This paper presents a Monte Carlo method for estimating the Dirichlet-to-Neumann (DtN) operator -- a boundary-to-boundary volumetric operator -- and its associated Steklov eigenmodes. We build on recent developments in Monte Carlo geometry processing by casting this boundary operator itself as the subject of estimation. The DtN operator, defined through a volumetric stochastic process, is then generalized to the exterior domain, where it couples disconnected components through the surrounding ambient space. We show that our method is orders of magnitude faster than existing boundary-element approaches for computing Steklov spectra while remaining robust to poor triangulations, high-resolution meshes, and multi-component geometry. To demonstrate this scalability, we compute interior and exterior Steklov eigenspectra for approximately 450,000 shapes from the uncurated Objaverse dataset. We incorporate these operators into Steklov-CLIP, a mesh-based neural network that uses volumetric spectral operators for large-scale contrastive 3D representation learning. The resulting network learns semantically meaningful global and dense shape representations, illustrating that geometrically-principled volumetric operators can be made practical at the scale of modern 3D datasets.

item.title: Cosine Misleads: Auxiliary Losses Reshape Vision Language Models, Not Their Latents
item.content:encoded: Latent visual reasoning (LVR) inserts supervised latent tokens between perception and answer generation in vision-language models (VLMs). The field uses alignment between these latents and their visual targets, i.e., cosine similarity or mean squared error (MSE), as both the training loss and the quality metric, assuming that better alignment yields a better answer. We test this with a designed matrix of five LVR variants and find the assumption inverted: cosine alignment is negatively correlated with accuracy across all five (r=-0.94). To explain this, we introduce PRISM, a pair of inference-time diagnostics: a linear probe that asks where the answer is decodable, and a corruption test that asks whether the latent is load-bearing. The supervised latents are largely bypassed. Corrupting them shifts accuracy by at most four points. The answer is decodable downstream of the latent but not at it, and the size of this decodability gap predicts how much each variant relies on its latent under perturbation. Consistent with an Information Bottleneck reading of the loss, the auxiliary objective reshapes the language model via shared parameters rather than via the latent variable it nominally optimizes.

item.title: GMBFormer: An NDVI-Guided Global Memory Bank Transformer for Urban Green-Space Extraction from Ultra-High-Resolution Imagery
item.content:encoded: Urban green-space extraction from ultra-high-resolution (UHR) imagery is commonly performed patch by patch, which limits semantic reuse among spatially separated but visually similar vegetation patterns. Directly injecting the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) into red-green-blue (RGB) backbones can also blur the roles of visual appearance learning and physical vegetation confidence. We propose GMBFormer, a SegFormer-based framework that replaces adjacency-driven feature propagation with selective, similarity-driven prototype retrieval. Only RGB channels enter the backbone and decoder, while NDVI is decoupled as a physics-informed gate that admits high-confidence vegetation descriptors into a compact global memory bank through momentum updates. During training and inference, the current patch queries stored prototypes through memory-mediated cross-attention, and the retrieved response is integrated with bounded overhead. Experiments use a self-constructed Chengdu UHR dataset with 7,700 labeled 512 x 512 patches and two reduced-label settings derived from the public International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ISPRS) Potsdam dataset. Under the same training and evaluation protocol, GMBFormer obtains mean intersection over union (mIoU)/mean Dice (mDice) scores of 89.25%/94.31%, 92.17%/95.92%, and 83.72%/90.86%, respectively, improving the controlled SegFormer-B4 baseline in each setting. Ablation studies indicate that decoupled NDVI admission, memory retrieval, capacity, and momentum jointly shape the final performance.

item.title: CAPR：一种高效的扩散语言模型强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
CAPR通过利用去噪轨迹实现高效强化学习，提升模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出CAPR（Cached-Amortized Path Refinement），一种基于扩散语言模型的强化学习算法。该方法通过总结去噪轨迹中的路径状态，生成廉价的兄弟延续，并通过块级价值头进行局部监督。在块级解码策略下，CAPR根据每个块中揭示的标记重新分配最终奖励，从而将稀疏奖励转化为块级PPO权重。实验表明，CAPR在Sudoku、Countdown、GSM8K和Math500等任务上取得了优于现有方法的结果，同时降低了计算成本，仅为传统平面回放的0.75倍，树状回放的0.6倍。

item.title: 空间语言模型：提升几何空间推理能力的多模态方法
item.content:encoded:
提出SLM模型，增强几何空间推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为空间语言模型（SLM）的多模态大语言模型，旨在解决传统大语言模型在空间推理中仅依赖符号化语言模式匹配的问题。SLM将位置信息作为第一类模态，直接操作学习到的空间表示，而非依赖文本描述。研究构建了空间指令数据集，并设计了空间评估基准SpatialEval，用于测试空间推理能力。实验表明，SLM在多个空间任务上优于基于符号推理的现有方法，展示了几何空间表示对鲁棒空间推理的优势。

item.title: 基于难度感知的SFT-RL框架提升小语言模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的SFT-RL训练框架，提升小语言模型推理效果。



摘要： 本文针对后训练阶段小语言模型（SLMs）的推理能力提升问题，提出了一种基于难度感知的SFT-then-RL框架。该框架根据SFT和RL的不同作用，将训练数据分为阶段特定的数据集。在SFT阶段，对于困难样本引入Bridge机制，将教师生成的推理过程转化为更易学习的监督信号；在RL阶段，对未解决的困难样本通过Critique Fine-Tuning进行诊断与修复，生成新的推理轨迹用于下一轮SFT。实验表明，该方法在多个推理基准上均优于现有基线方法，强调了SFT与RL阶段数据难度协调的重要性。

item.title: 动态对数级门控框架提升多模型融合效果
item.content:encoded:
DLLG通过动态对数级融合提升多模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出DLLG（动态对数级门控）框架，用于在不依赖token级标签或专家重训练的情况下，通过稀疏的响应级监督实现token级专家融合。该方法通过轻量级门控模块预测逐步融合权重，将轨迹级正确性与生成过程直接关联。实验表明，DLLG在多个推理和代码基准测试中均优于现有路由、启发式集成和参数合并方法，展示了学习到的对数级融合作为强大且可扩展的专家整合范式的潜力。

item.title: 基于认知行为疗法的抗阻心理辅导评估框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出抗阻心理辅导评估框架，提升LLM在真实对话中的表现。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型在心理辅导中的应用，指出当前评估基准因模拟客户快速转向配合而产生偏差。作者提出一种基于认知行为疗法的抗阻感知框架，引入CARS客户端模拟器和STREAMS双模块系统，通过强化学习优化战略推理与回应生成，并设计EWTS-MI熵加权指标评估高摩擦互动下的响应能力。实验验证了评估不匹配问题，并证明抗阻训练可增强模型在复杂辅导场景中的稳健性。

item.title: 联邦学习在多中心脓毒症早期预测中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
联邦学习实现隐私保护的多中心脓毒症预测模型。



摘要： 本文研究了联邦学习在多中心脓毒症早期预测中的应用，旨在解决医疗数据隐私敏感和分布式特性带来的挑战。实验数据来自中国三家三甲医院的648例临床样本，通过对比集中式训练和横向联邦学习框架，验证了联邦学习在保持高预测准确率的同时，有效避免了隐私泄露。进一步的隐私安全分析表明，攻击者无法从传输的模型参数中重建原始患者数据，证明该方法具有较强的抗数据重构能力。该研究为多中心医疗协作提供了安全、可行的解决方案。

item.title: 基于模型自评估的输出质量预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
模型可预测外部评委对其输出的评分，无需额外训练。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型是否能够预测外部评委对其输出的评分。研究发现，在未进行专门训练的情况下，基础模型通过少量示例即可准确预测多维度的质量评分。作者提出了一种名为Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE) 的方法，通过校准强化学习和掩码蒸馏两个阶段提升模型的自我评估能力，同时保持回答质量。实验表明，该方法在三个基准测试中表现出色，且评估结果具有跨评委的稳定性，表明其反映的是通用质量标准而非单一评委偏好。这为模型自评估提供了一种新的思路。

item.title: 无监督视频全景分割方法VideoCUPS的提出与评估
item.content:encoded:
提出首个无监督视频全景分割方法VideoCUPS，性能优于现有基线。



摘要： 本文介绍了无监督视频全景分割（VPS）任务，并提出了VideoCUPS，这是首个无需人工标注的VPS方法。该方法通过利用深度、运动和视觉线索生成时间一致的视频伪标签，并使用新的Video DropLoss进行训练，取得了优异的性能。研究还引入了全面的评估协议和四个竞争性基线，验证了VideoCUPS的有效性，展示了其在标签效率方面的优势，为未来无监督VPS研究奠定了基础。

item.title: 基于物理信息神经网络的污染物迁移模拟与反演分析
item.content:encoded:
H-PINN在污染物迁移模拟中表现更优，适用于复合防渗系统。



摘要： 本研究提出一种双域物理信息神经网络框架，用于模拟GCL/SL复合防渗系统中的污染物迁移。其中，GCL层采用稳态对流-弥散-生物降解模型，而底层土壤层则作为瞬态传输域进行建模。通过对比标准PINN（Std-PINN）和硬约束PINN（H-PINN）在不同渗滤液水头条件下的表现，发现H-PINN在早期传输阶段误差更小，预测更准确稳定，MAE和MRE均显著降低。此外，H-PINN在反演建模中可有效识别SL降解半衰期，具有良好的收敛性和抗噪能力。

item.content:encoded:
构建新型基准数据集以提升T细胞受体预测模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出两种互补的基准数据集，用于评估T细胞受体（TCR）抗原特异性预测模型的性能和泛化能力。现有模型在灵敏度和特异性方面存在不足，限制了其广泛应用。作者认为，这些新数据集不仅为模型评估提供了可靠框架，也为下一代TCR-抗原预测算法的发展奠定了基础。

item.title: AgentJet：一种用于大语言模型代理强化学习的分布式群体训练框架
item.content:encoded:
AgentJet 提供高效、灵活的多模型强化学习训练框架。



摘要： AgentJet 是一种分布式群体训练框架，专为大语言模型代理的强化学习设计。它采用解耦的多节点架构，将模型优化与代理执行分离，支持异构多模型训练、多任务混合训练、容错执行和实时代码迭代。该框架引入上下文跟踪模块以提升训练效率，并配备自动化研究系统，可在大规模集群上自主进行长期强化学习研究，无需人工干预。

item.title: 基于学习的骨结构角度自动测量方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自动骨结构角度测量方法，提升诊断准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于学习的骨结构角度自动测量方法，通过点候选生成和线模型提取轴参数，结合RANSAC和霍夫变换等技术提高鲁棒性。该方法在三种儿科临床任务中进行评估，分别在X光片、超声和髋关节发育不良评估中取得平均误差4.1°、5.4°和5.51°，优于传统基于关键点的方法，并已开源代码和标注数据。

item.title: 一种基于剪切梯度的差分隐私选择性发布算法
item.content:encoded:
提出DPSR-CG算法，兼顾隐私与模型性能。



摘要： 本文针对差分隐私随机梯度下降（DPSGD）在模型性能和收敛速度上的不足，提出了一种新的算法DPSR-CG。该算法通过严格的隐私分析，解决了现有DPSUR算法中因选择性发布机制导致的隐私保障不严谨问题。实验结果表明，DPSR-CG在多个数据集上均表现出优异的模型性能，同时保持了严格的隐私保护。研究强调了在隐私保护技术中对采样概率变化的重视，为后续相关研究提供了理论支持和实践参考。

item.title: 基于不确定性与个体行为的新冠防控策略模拟研究
item.content:encoded:
研究通过模拟个体行为与政策不确定性，验证了口罩和疫苗在控制疫情中的关键作用。



摘要： 本文提出一种整合疫情测量不确定性和政策执行不确定性的新方法，构建了一个包含1000名个体实时决策行为（如戴口罩、接种疫苗、购物）的模拟模型。同时，政策制定者根据健康和经济数据实施干预措施。该框架采用分层强化学习算法，结合深度Q网络及考虑不确定性的DDPG和TD3变体。模拟结果显示，口罩和疫苗在降低疫情峰值和持续时间方面效果显著，证明了将个体行为和不完美数据纳入公共卫生政策的重要性。

item.title: MetaPoint：实现图像生成中的精准空间控制
item.content:encoded:
MetaPoint通过特殊标记实现图像像素级空间控制。



摘要： 文章介绍了MetaPoint方法，解决生成式视觉模型在精确空间控制上的难题。该方法通过引入一个特殊标记来表示连续的2D坐标，利用模型原有的位置编码机制进行解析，无需修改架构即可实现对物体位置或边界框的精确控制。MetaPoint标记具有组合性，可作为空间基本单元，帮助规划代理将用户请求分解为结构化指令，提升生成式代理的能力和交互编辑系统的实用性。

item.title: 法律领域大模型代理的挑战与改进框架
item.content:encoded:
法律大模型代理在处理复杂案件时面临三大挑战，Parthenon框架提升其性能。



摘要： 随着大模型能力的提升，法律领域的AI代理有望将文档密集型任务转化为可审查的工作成果，但其可靠部署仍面临三大障碍：缺乏对当前最强模型组合在端到端法律任务中的行为证据、缺乏适应法律垂直领域的代理架构、以及在不断变化的环境中缺乏系统自我学习机制。本文通过一项大规模实证研究（涉及12,510个代理轨迹）发现，即使最先进的代理也难以在单次运行中完成任务，准确率随模型增强而提升，但任务完成度却停滞不前。随后，文章提出 extsc{Parthenon}框架，该框架将模型、工具、代理角色和法律知识等要素整合为可审计的界面，支持来源追溯、数据校准、交付合规及问题关闭。最后，通过反泄露学习循环，系统能根据失败评分自动优化技能、工具和知识，实现经验积累而不修改模型权重。实验表明，Parthenon显著提升了现有模型和工具在法律任务中的表现。

item.title: VISTA：解决UMI数据训练VLA模型的挑战
item.content:encoded:
VISTA框架提升机器人视觉语言动作模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出VISTA框架，旨在解决使用UMI数据训练大规模视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的挑战。文章指出，由于腕部鱼眼视角存在严重径向失真和局部夹爪中心视角，以及人类收集的轨迹常违反运动学限制，导致物理上不可行的动作。为此，VISTA通过三个协同组件进行改进：UMI-VQA数据集对齐视觉语言表示；物理验证流程筛选有效轨迹；两阶段联合训练提升视觉语言定位和动作预测能力。实验表明，VISTA在多种模拟和真实任务中优于现有基线模型。

item.title: 概念瓶颈模型的合成基准测试研究
item.content:encoded:
本文构建了概念瓶颈模型的合成基准，用于评估其在决策支持和自动化中的表现。



摘要： 本文提出了一种用于概念瓶颈模型的合成基准，旨在解决缺乏概念标签数据的问题。该基准支持两种主要应用场景：决策支持和自动化任务，并能控制影响模型性能的数据模态、概念选择、标注质量和完整性等因素。研究展示了如何利用这些基准评估不同类别的概念瓶颈模型，并通过实验揭示模型失败模式，为后续测试提供指导。

item.title: 乳腺癌多模态医疗大模型BreastGPT的构建与评估
item.content:encoded:
BreastGPT在乳腺癌全流程任务中表现优异，提升临床推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了针对乳腺癌诊疗流程设计的多模态医疗大模型BreastGPT。研究构建了包含186万条指令对的BreastStage数据集，涵盖5种影像模态和136种任务模板，用于评估多模态推理能力。基于此数据集，BreastGPT采用双分支视觉编码器和概念保持的token压缩技术，有效解决标准放射学与巨型病理图像之间的尺度差异。实验结果显示，BreastGPT在闭合式任务中达到75.66%的准确率，在开放式任务中获得89.92分，优于通用和专用医疗大模型。研究强调了流程对齐数据和跨尺度视觉建模对临床医学大模型的重要性。

item.title: 深度强化学习中的时间信用分配偏差及其机制分析
item.content:encoded:
研究发现深度强化学习中存在时间信用分配偏差，导致对高奖励峰值的偏好。



摘要： 本文探讨了深度强化学习中时间信用分配的问题，提出了一种称为Trace-Mediated Peak Bias（TMPB）的系统性失败模式。在中间资格轨迹深度下，智能体倾向于选择具有高奖励峰值的路径，而非总回报更高的路径。这解释了人类记忆中的峰终定律：人们更关注体验中最强烈的时刻。研究发现，这种偏差源于轨迹放大了远期时间差分误差，导致梯度冲击，而固定步长的随机梯度下降无法有效处理，从而引发全局高估。相反，自适应优化器通过二阶矩归一化缓解了这一问题。研究结果表明，人类类似的情境感知偏差可能自然地从分布式系统的信用分配数学约束中产生，且自适应优化是实现理性价值估计的理论必要条件。

item.title: 自反思API提升AI任务完成率与效率
item.content:encoded:
自反思API在验证失败时提供结构化建议，提高AI任务完成率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种自反思API机制，该机制在验证失败时返回结构化的恢复建议，使AI代理能够自主修复请求并重试。实验表明，在Anthropic模型上，这种结构化建议可将任务完成率提升36.7%至40%，同时提高每成功token的效率。然而，在gpt-4o-mini上未见显著提升。研究还发现，LLM基准测试中存在未记录的答案泄露问题，并提供了用于检测泄露的CI工具。代码和数据已开源。

item.title: 基于归一化流的扩散模型贝叶斯推断方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新归一化流架构用于学习扩散过程的转移密度函数。



摘要： 本文研究了从离散观测中进行扩散模型参数贝叶斯推断的主要挑战，即无法获得转移密度函数的解析表达式。为解决这一问题，作者提出了一种新的归一化流架构，在神经伽辽金框架下求解伴随的福克-普朗克方程，并以狄拉克质量作为初始条件。该方法特别适用于扩散矩阵在某些不可达边界区域为零的模型，如满足费勒条件的随机波动率模型。通过在观测轨迹上评估得到的转移密度函数，可以近似似然函数，从而实现高效的马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛后验采样。训练完成后，推理过程显著提高效率，无需实时求解福克-普朗克方程或依赖其他无需似然的方法。

item.title: 基于梯度损失的递归特征消除在肺癌分期中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出GL-RFE方法提升肺癌分期诊断准确率。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于梯度损失的递归特征消除（GL-RFE）框架，用于从胸部CT图像中提取关键放射组学特征，以提高肺癌分期的预测性能。通过深度神经网络计算特征对损失函数的梯度，实现特征重要性评估和递归剔除。实验结果显示，该方法在测试集上达到90.22%的准确率，优于传统特征选择方法。可视化分析进一步验证了特征冗余减少和类别可分性增强。该方法适用于高维、小样本的生物医学数据，具有良好的可重复性和解释性。

item.title: 多视频摘要中的位置偏差研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多视频摘要中位置偏差对摘要质量的影响。



摘要： 本文探讨了多视频摘要中位置偏差的问题，即同一视频在不同输入位置时摘要质量会发生变化。研究基于ActivityNet和新闻视频构建了基准数据集，涵盖烹饪、家庭、休闲和新闻场景，并测试了九种开源和专有多模态大语言模型。通过覆盖度、方向性位置偏差和中间-边缘差距三个指标评估位置效应，发现位置偏差具有领域和模型依赖性，增加视觉或生成预算并不能完全消除不平衡。研究还分析了提示级别的缓解方法，强调多视频摘要对输入协议和位置的敏感性，呼吁更鲁棒的无序多模态系统。

item.title: 深度无监督域适应中的嵌入验证方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无偏目标风险估计方法提升域适应性能。



摘要： 本文针对深度无监督域适应（Deep UDA）中缺乏准确标准化模型选择方法的问题，提出了一种名为Deep Embedded Validation（DEV）的新方法。该方法通过将适应后的特征表示嵌入验证过程，实现对目标风险的无偏估计，并利用控制变量技术进一步降低方差。理论和实验结果均验证了该方法的有效性，有助于推动Deep UDA领域的进一步发展。

item.title: 非凸神经网络中平坦性与泛化能力的关系研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示非凸神经网络中平坦性与泛化能力的联系。



摘要： 本文探讨了在非凸二层同质神经网络中，平坦性与模型泛化能力之间的关系。尽管已有研究表明网络对称性可以改变平坦度而不影响损失函数，但作者发现，在特定条件下，最平坦的插值器仍能实现良好的泛化性能。研究指出，对于由单指标模型组成的合成数据，若近似误差和标签噪声较低，最平坦的插值器能够达到较小的总体损失，从而证明平坦性与泛化之间存在直接联系。该结论适用于多种激活函数和现实数据分布。

item.title: 基于群体相对策略优化的偏见缓解方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出BiasGRPO框架，有效缓解大模型中的社会偏见。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型中的社会偏见问题，提出了一种基于群体相对策略优化（BiasGRPO）的框架。与传统方法相比，BiasGRPO通过在多个生成结果之间进行相对奖励归一化，提高了对齐稳定性并保留了在线训练的探索优势。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中优于DPO和PPO。同时，作者扩展了一个跨领域的数据集，并开发了一个高效且避免知识退化的偏见奖励模型，可直接用于多目标RLHF流程。

item.title: REGAIN框架：优化预测协调中的辅助测量选择
item.content:encoded:
REGAIN通过选择互补信息的辅助测量提升预测协调效果。



摘要： 本文提出REGAIN框架，旨在优化预测协调中辅助测量的选择。不同于传统方法，REGAIN学习归一化的辅助方向，利用冻结的预测模型进行预测，并根据目标加权损失减少来选择方向。研究指出，有效的辅助方向应提供未解决目标不确定性的补充信息，而非仅易于预测。分析揭示了协方差风险降低机制、偏差变化对实际二次风险的影响及增益信号的稳定性。实验表明，该方法在PM2.5和旅游数据中能提升多变量和分层预测效果，尤其在揭示原始系统未捕捉的残余不确定性时表现突出。

item.title: 基于视觉皮层组织的图像到fMRI编码框架CHASMBrain
item.content:encoded:
提出CHASMBrain框架，提升图像到fMRI映射性能。



摘要： 本文提出CHASMBrain，一种用于图像到fMRI编码的分层两阶段框架。该框架采用双流Mamba结构，分别处理全局语义标记和局部空间块，模仿视觉皮层的功能组织。实验表明，该方法在NSD数据集上取得0.429的皮尔逊相关系数和0.261的均方误差，优于所有基线模型。研究还发现，局部流与初级视觉皮层相关，而CLS流则贡献更广泛的语义信息，且模型具有跨被试泛化能力。

item.title: AutoLab：评估长期迭代优化能力的新基准
item.content:encoded:
AutoLab挑战模型在长时间内的持续优化能力，强调迭代与反馈的重要性。



摘要： 文章介绍了AutoLab，一个用于评估长期闭环优化能力的新基准。该基准包含36个跨四个领域的任务，要求模型在有限时间内不断改进初始不完美的方案。实验表明，成功的关键在于模型的持续迭代和反馈整合能力，而非初始表现。尽管Claude-Opus-4.6表现出色，但多数前沿模型在时间限制下进展有限。研究强调了时间意识和持续优化在自主代理中的重要性，并开源了所有相关资源以推动研究。

item.title: Archi: Agentic Operations at the CMS Experiment
item.content:encoded: We present Archi, an open-source, end-to-end framework for scientific collaborations that combines the systematic ingestion and organization of heterogeneous data sources with the deployment of configurable, private, and extensible agents that retrieve and reason over them. An instance of Archi has been deployed for the Computing Operations team of the CMS experiment at CERN's LHC since February 2026 as a support agent for technical operators, offering retrieval and analysis capabilities by combining documentation, historical data, and live monitoring systems. We evaluate the system on operator feedback and a question set collected from production usage, graded by human and automated panels. The system proves effective at operational tasks, resolving real-world queries posed by CMS operators. We also observe that locally-hosted, open-weight models perform competitively, enabling fully private management of sensitive data.

item.title: The Right Measure for Physics-Constrained Generation: A Co-Area Correction for Posterior-Consistent PDE Inverse Problems
item.content:encoded: Generative models -- diffusion and flow matching -- are increasingly used to solve partial differential equation (PDE) inverse problems, enforcing the governing physics as a \emph{hard constraint} (via projection or guidance) and reporting the resulting samples as a Bayesian posterior with calibrated uncertainty. We show that this widely adopted recipe samples the wrong distribution. Conditioning a generative prior on a hard PDE constraint is conditioning on a measure-zero manifold -- an operation that is intrinsically ambiguous (the Borel--Kolmogorov paradox) and whose physically correct resolution, the small-residual-noise limit, carries a co-area (Fixman) Jacobian factor $[det(JJ^{\top})]^{-1/2}$ that projection- and guidance-based methods silently omit. We make the bias precise, show that it grows with the heterogeneity of the constraint sensitivity, and validate it on controlled problems against an \emph{i.i.d.} ground-truth arbiter. The omitted factor is not a second-order detail: removing it inflates the posterior error to $20\times$ the sampling-noise floor; minimal-displacement projection (as in PCFM) is biased at $9\times$ the floor; and a naive scalar reweighting does not fix it. We introduce \textbf{CoCoS}, a measure-aware constrained sampler that targets the correct co-area posterior, and show that it matches the gold-standard posterior to within sampling noise. Our results imply that ``satisfying the physics'' is not the same as ``sampling the posterior,'' and give a principled correction for uncertainty-aware scientific inference.

item.title: Policy Gradient for Continuous-Time Robust Markov Decision Processes
item.content:encoded: The framework of robust Markov decision processes (RMDPs) allows the design of reinforcement learning agents that satisfy performance guarantees under worst-case transition dynamics. Traditional RMDPs consider discrete-time dynamics and recently, sample-efficient policy gradient algorithms have been considered in this context. This paper investigates policy gradient algorithms within a continuous-time RMDP framework. Policy gradients and adversarial gradients are derived using pathwise and adjoint-based formulas for stochastic and ordinary differential equations. We propose double-loop optimisers to obtain linear convergence in the oracle-based setting and an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{1}{ε^2})$ sample complexity in the sample-based setting in an analysis which also derives novel tools for the framework of undiscounted total cost MDPs. Additionally, we propose mean-field optimisers as distributional optimisers with an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{1}{K})$ oracle-based convergence rate and an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{N^2}ε)$ sample complexity under $N$-particle approximation. The effectiveness of continuous-time policy gradient algorithms is confirmed for both optimisers on continuous-time RMDPs with neural ordinary differential equation dynamics.

item.title: VCIFBench: Evaluating Complex Instruction Following for Video Understanding
item.content:encoded: Multimodal large language models have made rapid progress in video understanding, yet existing benchmarks largely rely on simple prompts and provide limited evidence about whether models can satisfy explicit output constraints. We introduce VCIFBench, a benchmark for evaluating complex instruction following in video understanding. VCIFBench constructs constraint-rich instructions from both benchmark-adapted and directly video-grounded prompts, covering content, format, style, and structure requirements, and evaluates model outputs with a hybrid verification pipeline. The benchmark contains 306 satisfiable test instructions, a 540-pair DPO preference dataset, and a 30-item conflict diagnostic subset. Experiments on 10 MLLMs show that joint constraint satisfaction remains challenging. We further show that DPO training on VCIFBench data can improve instruction-following performance.

item.title: AIP: A Graph Representation for Learning and Governing Agent Skills
item.content:encoded: Agent Skills today consist largely of free-form prose requiring the agent to read, interpret, and re-derive how to act in every session. This imposes two compounding costs: reduced reliability on implementation-heavy tasks, and difficulty in skill creation and improvement, since editing prose is a fragile process that both humans and agents struggle with, particularly for domain-specific procedural knowledge underrepresented in model training. The Agent Instruction Protocol (AIP) addresses both by modeling a skill as a directed execution graph: discrete steps as nodes backed by deterministic scripts or natural-language descriptions, connected by explicit typed input/output edges, and governed by a schema-validated YAML specification. A compiler meta-skill translates existing human-written skills into this form. The benefits are twofold. First, compiling human-written skills to AIP raised Claude Sonnet's mean task reward from 0.60 to 0.71 and pass rate from 53% to 67% across 27 real agent tasks from SkillsBench - a statistically significant gain (Wilcoxon signed-rank p = 0.011), winning 12 tasks to 2 with 13 ties - often in less wall-clock time. The graph delivers vetted, runnable units to the agent rather than asking it to re-derive code, commands, and tool calls from natural language. Second, on creation and improvement, because each skill is schema-validated, functionally testable, and addressable node-by-node, failures can be diagnosed and repaired precisely. Two authored-skill failures were traced to the script level. After adjusting the AIP spec and recompiling, both recovered with zero regressions (one task going from 0/5 to 5/5), turning skill improvement into a measurable tuning loop rather than a prose rewrite. That same graph structure supports corpus-level governance and skill introspection, and provides a natural action space for reinforcement learning over skills.

item.title: STaR-Quant: State-Time Consistent Post-Training Quantization for Diffusion Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Diffusion large language models (DLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive LLMs by generating text through iterative masked denoising with bidirectional context. However, their large model sizes and iterative denoising process introduce substantial memory and computational overhead, motivating post-training quantization for efficient deployment. In this paper, we identify two key challenges for low-bit DLLM quantization: state-dependent activation disparity and temporal error accumulation. Masked and unmasked tokens exhibit different activation distributions within each denoising step, while quantization errors can accumulate across steps during iterative decoding. To address these challenges, we propose STaR-Quant, a state-time consistent PTQ framework for DLLMs. STaR-Quant introduces State-Guided Activation Transformation (SGAT) to assign masked and unmasked tokens to different activation transformation spaces with a unified static weight-side transformation. It further introduces Temporal Attention Compensation (TAC) to correct the quantized attention representation via a lightweight block-diagonal affine mapping. Experiments on representative DLLMs demonstrate that STaR-Quant consistently improves low-bit weight-activation quantization over strong PTQ baselines, while delivering up to 1.69x speedup and 3.14x memory saving over FP16 deployment.

item.title: Learning What Not to Impute: An Uncertainty-Aware Diffusion Framework for Meaningful Missingness
item.content:encoded: Missing value imputation is a fundamental task in machine learning, with most existing methods assuming that all missing entries correspond to unobserved regular values. In many real-world datasets, however, missingness may arise from two distinct sources: some entries are meaningfully missing (intrinsically absent and semantically valid), while others are missing due to the observation process and should be imputed. We formalize this distinction as a selective imputation problem, where the goal is to jointly infer which missing entries should be preserved and which should be recovered. To address this challenge, we propose Diff-Joint, a diffusion-based framework that jointly models tabular data together with a latent missingness mask. The method alternates between conditional sampling and uncertainty-aware aggregation to iteratively refine both imputed values and missingness labels. Empirical results on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that Diff-Joint effectively identifies meaningfully missing entries while achieving competitive imputation accuracy and improved downstream task performance.

item.title: MemoryDocDataSet: A Benchmark for Joint Conversational Memory and Long Document Reasoning
item.content:encoded: AI systems increasingly need to combine two demanding capabilities: navigating multi-session conversation history and performing deep reading comprehension within long documents. Yet no existing benchmark evaluates both simultaneously. We introduce MemoryDocDataSet, a synthetic benchmark of 50 micro-worlds and 1,000 QA pairs in which each instance comprises 3-5 personas, a temporal event graph spanning months of activity, 3-5 real long documents (20,000-50,000 tokens each sourced from the Caselaw Access Project), multi-session conversations grounded on those documents, and 20 question-answer pairs across five reasoning categories. The defining feature is the Hybrid source tag: questions requiring a system to first navigate conversation history to identify which document is relevant, then extract the answer from within that document. Hybrid questions account for 75.1% of the dataset. Dataset quality is characterised through a prompt-sensitivity self-consistency analysis using LLM-as-judge, yielding a median Cohen's $κ= 0.634$ across all 50 micro-worlds. We evaluate six baseline configurations spanning truncated context, long-context LLMs, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and memory systems. The best baseline (RAG-Both) achieves 0.358 overall F1 and 0.342 on Hybrid. Document-only retrieval (RAG-Doc) collapses to 0.267 on Hybrid despite achieving 0.453 on Doc-only questions, demonstrating a clear joint-retrieval gap that motivates architectures unifying conversational memory with long-document navigation. We release the dataset, generation pipeline, and all baseline implementations.

item.title: Learning Control-Affine Reduced-Order Models via Autoencoders
item.content:encoded: We present in this paper a framework for the identification of control-affine reduced-order models (ROMs). The proposed method utilizes autoencoders (AEs) to transform the high-dimensional states, and potentially the high-dimensional inputs, into reduced latent ones suitable for control-affine state-space dynamics. This is achieved by simultaneous training of the AE and the state-space model. In addition, we extend the discrete ROM formulation to a sequence-based model, which processes state and input histories to improve prediction accuracy while preserving the control-affine structure. We motivate our framework by applying feedback linearization to the derived models, and we present guidelines for its efficient use. The proposed framework is assessed on two numerical examples and its performance is compared to a baseline model, where the AE identifies a latent space with linear state-space dynamics. The assessment involves evaluating the prediction accuracy of the ROM on test data and its effectiveness in controlling the system to a desired state or trajectory.

item.title: GRAIL: Gradient-Reweighted Advantages for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (e.g. GRPO) is now a common way to improve mathematical reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs). However, current methods usually broadcast one sequence-level advantage to all tokens, or use costly process reward models (PRMs) for step-level supervision. Uniform advantage distribution assumes that all tokens contribute equally to the final reward. This dilutes the gradient signal, since flawed reasoning steps and filler words are updated as strongly as valid logical inferences. To address this, we introduce Gradient-Reweighted Advantage (GRAIL), an intrinsic token-wise advantage reweighting method. GRAIL uses gradient-activation saliency to place more weight on tokens that are more locally sensitive to the final answer. Evaluations across five models from the Qwen3, R1-distilled and OctoThinker families show that GRAIL consistently outperforms GRPO. GRAIL achieved an average improvement of 3.60% in accuracy and 3.05% in Pass@3, demonstrating that fine-grained reasoning alignment can be achieved without process-level supervision.

item.title: CleanCodec: Efficient and Robust Speech Tokenization via Perceptually Guided Encoding
item.content:encoded: Neural audio codecs are a key component of speech processing pipelines, compressing audio into discrete tokens for downstream modeling. However, existing codecs struggle to balance reconstruction quality with token efficiency, often encoding perceptually irrelevant information such as background noise and recording artifacts at the expense of linguistically and acoustically meaningful content. We reframe audio tokenization as a selective information bottleneck problem and propose CleanCodec, a denoising audio codec which learns to encode only perceptually important features and discard imperceptible information. At just 12.5 tokens per second, CleanCodec achieves state-of-the-art tokenization efficiency, substantially outperforming existing codecs in speaker similarity and speech intelligibility. Evaluations on downstream text-to-speech and voice conversion tasks further demonstrate improved performance and up to 17x faster inference, highlighting significant efficiency gains.

item.title: Self-Evolving Deep Research via Joint Generation and Evaluation
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become increasingly adopted in daily applications, with deep research standing out as a particularly important capability. Unlike traditional question-answering (QA) tasks, deep research report generation lacks definitive ground-truth, making reward design inherently unverifiable and limiting effective reinforcement learning. Existing approaches mitigate this challenge with LLM-as-a-judge and query-dependent evaluation rubrics, but they still rely on static evaluators that cannot adapt their standards as the solver improves, leading to insufficient and eventually saturated optimization pressure. We address this limitation with a \textbf{s}elf-evolving \textbf{co}-evolutionary training framework for deep \textbf{re}search evaluation and generation (SCORE), which tightly couples an evaluator and a solver in a shared-parameter learning process. Rather than treating generation and evaluation as isolated modules, we leverage their intrinsic connection to enable joint improvement within a single shared-parameter model. To restrict this process, we introduce a meta-harness, which dynamically controls the evaluation environment based on solver performance, encouraging valid evaluation dimensions and sufficiently deep evaluator search. Extensive experiments on deep research benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvement in report generation quality, showing that co-evolving evaluation and generation is a promising direction for training open-ended research agents.

item.title: Plan, Watch, Recover: A Benchmark and Architectures for Proactive Procedural Assistance
item.content:encoded: We envision a proactive multi-modal assistant system which gives users real-time step-by-step guidance on a procedural task, autonomously deciding \textit{when} to interrupt, and \textit{how} to coach. However, progress is limited by the absence of large-scale, cross-domain benchmarks that reflect realistic conditions, particularly the common case in which users deviate from the expected step sequence. We address this gap with four contributions: \textbf{(1)}~we release \textbf{EgoProactive}, a large-scale wearable-egocentric dataset for proactive procedural assistance with explicit Out-of-Plan (OOP) annotations and recovery steps; \textbf{(2)}~we augment five established benchmarks (Ego4D, EPIC-KITCHENS, EgoExo4D, HoloAssist, HowTo100M) into \textbf{Pro\textsuperscript{2}Bench} under a unified proactive-guidance schema; \textbf{(3)}~we propose a \textbf{decoupled planner--interaction architecture} specialized for procedural state, visual cues, and recovery injection; \textbf{(4)}~we introduce a post-training recipe that transfers across model families, validated by cross-backbone replication on Llama~4 and Qwen-3.6-VL. In extensive experiments, our trained Llama-4 system substantially improves objective intervention quality over strong proprietary baselines (Claude Opus~4.6, Gemini~3.1~Pro, GPT~5.2) and open-weight baselines (Qwen3~VL~235B) baselines across all six datasets. Oracle-plan experiments further show that, when plan quality is controlled, the trained duplex model produces high-quality guidance and large gains on Out-of-Plan recovery.

item.title: COMBINER: Composed Image Retrieval Guided by Attribute-based Neighbor Relations
item.content:encoded: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) represents a challenging retrieval task that targets locating specific images through multimodal inputs. Despite recent progress in CIR techniques, prior approaches often overlook cases where images appear visually alike yet differ in attributes, potentially undermining both multimodal feature fusion and similarity modeling. To mitigate this limitation, we design a unified representation of cross-modal features based on attribute prototypes. Nevertheless, the task is far from straightforward, owing to three core issues: (1) entanglement in attribute-level semantics, (2) inconsistency across modalities, and (3) supervised signal missing. To tackle the above obstacles, we introduce a COMposed image retrieval network guided By attrIbute-based NEighbor Relations (COMBINER). Specifically, we first design an Adaptive Semantic Disentanglement module, which is capable of disentangling attribute features based on multimodal primitive features. Secondly, we propose a Unified Prototype-based Composition module, which can construct cross-modal unified prototypes (CUP) and facilitate multimodal feature composition. Finally, we introduce a Dual Relations Modeling module, which can mine pairwise and neighbor relations based on attribute similarity. Compared to traditional neighbor relations modeling CIR methods, COMBINER represents the first study addressing the phenomenon of visually similar but attribute-unrelated samples. It achieves a more accurate understanding of the semantic relations among samples by employing an attribute prototype-based similarity metric. Comprehensive experiments conducted on three benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness of our proposed COMBINER. The implementation of our method will be accessed at https://github.com/Lee-zixu/COMBINER

item.title: RePercENT: Scaling Disentangled Representation Learning Beyond Two Modalities
item.content:encoded: To leverage the full potential of multimodal data, we need representations that go beyond the state-of-the-art alignment and fusion approaches and exploit all cross-modal interactions without sacrificing modality-specific information. Learning disentangled representations is a principled way to identify these underlying shared and unique factors that are hidden in observational data. However, while multimodal disentanglement is a compelling paradigm, existing methods are largely confined to the two-modality regime due to its inherent scalability bottleneck. To address this, we propose RePercENT, a self-supervised framework designed to surpass these limitations and unlocks scalable pairwise disentanglement beyond two modalities. Through a multimodal `plug-and-play' architecture, our approach operates directly on pre-extracted embeddings, eliminating the need for extensive joint pre-training while making no assumptions regarding the underlying modalities or foundation model backbones. Moreover, we introduce a joint optimization objective for simultaneously deriving the shared and unique components, and provide formal theoretical guarantees that characterize the optimality of our solution. Across diverse modalities and tasks, RePercENT successfully recovers disentangled components while maintaining competitive performance and significantly reducing computational complexity.

item.title: RowNet: A Memory Transformer for Tabular Regression
item.content:encoded: Real estate valuation is a structured regression problem in which prices are governed by heterogeneous feature types, sparse regional effects, nonlinear interactions, and the practical logic of comparable properties. Standard multilayer perceptrons treat each row as an isolated vector and must learn locality, scale sensitivity, and categorical matching from supervision alone. Gradient-boosted decision trees provide strong tabular baselines, but their feature-centric splitting mechanism does not explicitly model the retrieval of similar historical observations. This paper presents RowNet, a retrieval-based neural architecture for real estate price-per-square-meter prediction. RowNet represents a query property through pairwise similarity features against a memory bank of labeled properties. A first retrieval layer estimates a coarse target from feature-only similarities. A second layer augments the memory comparison with target-consistency features and uses multiple learned attention heads to retrieve complementary comparable sets. A final mixture-of-experts module combines learned gating, residual correction, entropy regularization, and head-diversity regularization to produce the prediction.

item.title: Beyond Symmetric Alignment: Spectral Diagnostics of Modality Imbalance in Vision-Language Models in the Medical Domain
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle when applied to medical image-text data, yet the tools available to diagnose this failure remain limited. Existing representation alignment metrics are symmetric, collapsing both modalities into a single score and hiding which modality drives cross-modal degradation. We introduce the Spectral Alignment Score (SAS), an asymmetric metric that projects both modalities onto the principal eigenbasis of an anchor modality and computes eigenvalue-weighted per-eigenmode correlations, resulting in directional scores whose difference quantifies modality information imbalance. We embed SAS within a benchmarking framework evaluating 15 VLMs across natural and medical image-text datasets alongside 6 alignment metrics and bidirectional retrieval. Our experiments show that medical images retain richer structural information than their paired clinical reports, a directional asymmetry invisible to all competing metrics, and that SAS achieves the strongest zero-label correlation with retrieval performance in the medical domain, positioning it as a practical diagnostic tool for clinical deployment. Code is available at this URL: https://github.com/iamalegambetti/medical-vlms-assessment.

item.title: NextMotionQA: Benchmarking and Judging Human Motion Understanding with Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Reliable evaluation of human motion understanding is fundamental to advancing embodied AI, robotics, and animation. However, existing benchmarks suffer from coarse semantic granularity, undifferentiated difficulty, limited annotation quality, and pervasive answer ambiguity, leaving them unable to diagnose where current models fail. To bridge this gap, we introduce NextMotionQA, a comprehensive benchmark that leverages vision-language models (VLMs) for semi-automated, expert-verified dataset. NextMotionQA features three complementary tasks: multiple-choice question answering, video captioning, and fine-grained error correction. Each task is systematically structured across three core semantic axes and stratified into three task complexity levels. Our extensive evaluation of twelve representative VLMs uncovers critical capability gaps and weakness that remain invisible under conventional, single-task evaluations. In a complementary direction, recent work has begun using VLMs as judges for text-to-motion evaluation; we ask whether they show the same degradation under harder tasks. We find that VLMs align strongly with expert ratings on coarse criteria (Cohen's κ=0.70) but break down on fine-grained, part-level judgment (κ=0.10), validating the paradigm in its strong regime while clarifying its limits.

item.title: HYolo: An Intelligent IoT-Based Object Detection System Using Hypergraph Learning
item.content:encoded: This paper presents HYolo, an intelligent IoT-based object detection framework that integrates hypergraph learning into the YOLO architecture. Traditional YOLO-based object detection models primarily capture pairwise feature interactions and may fail to model complex high-order relationships among objects and contextual features. To address this limitation, HYolo incorporates hypergraph learning to capture richer contextual dependencies and improve object representation. Experimental evaluation on the COCO dataset demonstrates significant performance improvements over baseline YOLO models. The proposed approach achieves approximately 12% improvement in mAP@50 while enhancing overall detection accuracy and robustness. By modeling high-order feature relationships, HYolo provides improved contextual understanding and more reliable object detection performance in IoT-based environments. The results indicate that integrating hypergraph learning into object detection pipelines offers a promising direction for intelligent and context-aware IoT vision systems.

item.title: SAID: Accelerating Diffusion-Based Language Models via Scaffold-Aware Iterative Decoding
item.content:encoded: Diffusion large language models (DLLMs) enable non-autoregressive generation by iteratively denoising corrupted token sequences with bidirectional context. Despite their ability to update multiple positions in parallel, inference remains costly due to the many denoising steps required for high-quality generation. We propose SAID, a Scaffold-Aware Iterative Decoding framework that accelerates DLLMs by reallocating computation across tokens. SAID first spends denoising computation on scaffold tokens to establish the coarse semantic structure, and then completes predictable detail tokens with fewer steps. We further adapt SAID to block-wise diffusion decoding and introduce Confidence-Hierarchical Layered Generation (CHLG), which assigns additional steps only to low-confidence tokens. Experiments on LLaDA-8B and LLaDA 1.5 across math, coding, and knowledge benchmarks show that SAID significantly accelerates DLLM inference with a maximum speedup of 9.1x while maintaining competitive performance. Our code is publicly available: https://github.com/TH-AI-Lab-PKU/SAID.

item.title: 大型语言模型在消费设备维修任务中的评估与挑战
item.content:encoded:
LLMs在设备维修中表现有限，需加强安全防护。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在消费设备维修任务中的应用，指出该领域虽重要但研究不足。维修任务涉及不完整描述、硬件诊断、故障排除和安全决策，错误建议可能导致设备损坏或数据丢失。研究引入了一个包含991个真实维修问题的基准数据集，并提供孟加拉语翻译以评估跨语言性能。评估结果显示，尽管LLMs能提供一定帮助，但在高风险维修任务中仍不可靠，需要严格的评估和安全措施。手机维修是最具挑战性和安全性要求最高的领域，所有模型在电路板诊断、维修优先级和安全恢复方面均出现重大错误。孟加拉语响应整体表现低于英语响应，GPT-5.4在所有模型中表现最佳。

item.title: 提升多模态大语言模型的对抗鲁棒性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究如何增强多模态大语言模型对视觉对抗扰动的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文系统研究了多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在面对视觉对抗扰动时的鲁棒性问题。通过引入诊断性CLIP对齐协议，揭示了大规模多模态对抗预训练是实现强鲁棒性迁移的关键因素。实验表明，将鲁棒视觉编码器整合到MLLM中可显著提升图像描述和视觉问答任务的性能。此外，研究还发现直接对非鲁棒MLLM进行对抗训练会损害其性能，而从鲁棒基础模型出发进行端到端训练可进一步提升效果。测试阶段的轻量级视觉随机变换也能有效提升非鲁棒模型的对抗性能，并显著减少白盒攻击下的有害内容生成。

item.title: GTR：一种几何感知的持续强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
GTR通过几何感知信任区域提升非平稳环境下的强化学习性能。



摘要： 本文指出，尽管PPO在静态环境中表现优异，但在持续和非平稳环境下效果不佳。原因在于其局部更新方向效率低下，缺乏对行为变化的几何引导。虽然基于差异的正则化部分解决了几何感知问题，但其单调增加的惩罚会抑制必要的策略变化。为此，作者提出GTR方法，利用高斯核重塑信任区域，实现局部稳定性和渐进放松，同时引入混合高斯锚点减少方差。GTR在多种任务中表现出色，表明几何感知的信任区域设计是提升复杂非平稳环境下强化学习性能的可行方向。

item.title: A²方法提升视觉分类中的目标定位与特征提取
item.content:encoded:
A²方法结合小模型定位与大模型特征提取，提升分类性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为A²的方法，旨在结合小规模自监督ViT在目标定位上的优势与大规模ViT在特征提取上的能力。通过将小模型的注意力图用于定位，再用大模型对定位区域进行特征提取，A²实现了良好的定位效果和丰富的特征表示。该方法无需额外标注或训练，使用预训练特征，在多个基准测试中表现优异，尤其在分布偏移情况下优于端到端注意力训练方法。

item.title: 基于评分标准的多任务微调Transformer模型用于C++编程作业自动评分
item.content:encoded:
多任务微调提升C++作业自动评分与教师评分一致性。



摘要： 本文研究了基于评分标准的多任务Transformer模型微调方法，用于自动化评分C++编程作业。通过整合学期数据、评分和评分标准，构建统一输入序列，采用BART模型进行训练，结合分布匹配项以增强评分分布的一致性。实验对比了单任务与多任务、硬标签与软标签、有无评分标准等不同条件，结果显示多任务BART在边界软标签和评分标准指导下表现最优，优于传统基线模型。此外，全微调T5进一步提升了分布一致性，而配对预训练则在减少数值误差的同时增加了对少数类的敏感性。整体表明，基于评分标准的校准训练能更贴近教师评分行为。

item.title: ZX-Calculus：依赖类型理论的扩展与信念修正整合
item.content:encoded:
文章提出ZX-Calculus，扩展MLTT并整合路径类型、非单调语义和构造性AGM信念修正。



摘要： 本文提出ZX-Calculus，作为Martin-Lof依赖类型理论（MLTT）的保守扩展，融合了路径索引类型、预层非单调语义以及构造性AGM信念修正。通过Coq形式化验证了34个完整证明，其中两个核心结论未被接受。文章分四个部分展开：(I) 路径类型定义执行轨迹的归纳家族，并证明可及性对应关系和确定性重放；(II) 引入基于路径的命题的反变层语义，区分证明论单调性与语义非单调性；(III) 提出构造性部分交收缩算法，验证所有八条AGM公理；(IV) 揭示B^AGM在序列修正中违反层合成律，引入单步修正系统（SSRS）以解决路径依赖信念修正与函子一致性的矛盾。

item.title: 基于几何结构的图预训练模型研究
item.content:encoded:
探索图结构的可迁移性，提出GAUGE模型提升图任务性能。



摘要： 本文研究了图结构在预训练-微调范式中的可迁移性问题。不同于传统方法关注离散子结构，作者从功能行为角度出发，结合黎曼几何理论，提出一种新的图内在几何学习框架Neural Vector Bundle。在此基础上设计了GAUGE模型，通过构建向量丛、扁平化几何兼容局部坐标以及引入Dirichlet损失函数，有效提升了零样本链接预测和图同构等任务的性能。

item.title: 生态数据主动学习的评估与优化方法
item.content:encoded:
主动学习在生态数据标注中面临评估偏差，需优化发现稀有类别的能力。



摘要： 文章指出，当前生态数据主动学习的评估方式主要依赖预测性能，但这种评估方式与实际生态任务目标不匹配。生态任务更关注高效地对整个数据集进行转导标注，而不仅仅是预测准确性。研究强调了人类参与的重要性，特别是在标注长尾类别（如稀有物种）时。作者提出一种新的采样难度度量，并引入基于生态稀疏曲线的保守混合停止标准，以提高对稀有类别的识别效率，从而改善生态监测效果。

item.title: EvoMemNav：一种高效的零样本具身导航记忆框架
item.content:encoded:
EvoMemNav提升零样本具身导航性能，通过细粒度记忆图实现更准确的环境理解与决策。



摘要： 本文提出EvoMemNav，一种高效、自进化、细粒度的记忆框架，用于零样本具身导航。该框架构建了视觉-语义记忆图（VSMGraph），保留原始视图作为核心记忆，并通过轻量级语义提示和拓扑关系组织为房间-视图-对象层次结构，以保留细粒度细节用于消歧和停止验证。为适应不断增长的记忆，引入了预算化的粗到细策略，先压缩搜索空间，再针对性地调用视觉语言模型进行验证。此外，EvoMemNav在每个子任务后进行反射驱动的写回，更新附带先验知识的图结构，以优化未来决策而无需重新训练。实验表明，在GOAT-Bench和HM3D数据集上，EvoMemNav在SR/SPL指标上表现优异，具有更好的多实例消歧能力、更少的提前停止和更强的零样本泛化能力。

item.title: 结合视觉模拟与抽象推理的可控推理方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合视觉模拟与抽象推理的新方法，提升任务准确性。



摘要： 本文研究如何将世界模型生成的视觉未来模拟与多模态大语言模型的抽象推理相结合，以提高任务决策的准确性。作者提出了可控的具象推理框架，并构建了两个基准测试集用于评估方法效果。通过使用特权未来信息进行训练，该方法在两个基准测试中分别提升了10.6%和10.9%，同时增强了对噪声或冲突模拟的鲁棒性。相关代码和数据集已公开。

item.title: 基于模型的对抗鲁棒强化学习后处理方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需额外训练的深度强化学习后处理方法以提升鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文研究如何在对抗性环境扰动下提升强化学习（RL）的实际应用能力，特别关注将对抗鲁棒RL与基于模型的RL结合。作者提出了一种在推理阶段进行后处理的鲁棒化方法，利用已学习的模型和训练好的策略进行鲁棒策略优化，无需重新训练神经网络。该方法通过在受限不确定性集内使用投影梯度下降近似对抗性轨迹，并考虑并缓解分布外问题。实验结果显示，在受扰动的Gymnasium MuJoCo环境中，该方法显著提升了模型的鲁棒性，同时考虑了推理阶段的计算限制。

item.title: 基于神经算子的频域扩散后验采样方法
item.content:encoded:
结合神经算子与扩散后验采样的方法，解决高频率信息丢失问题。



摘要： 本文提出一种结合神经算子（NO）与扩散后验采样（DPS）的方法，以克服NO在高频内容上的系统性衰减问题。该方法利用NO作为辅助观测，在扩散先验基础上进行后验采样，并通过频谱加权引导得分来校准NO的频率依赖准确性。实验表明，该方法在3D弹性波场预测任务中有效减少高频偏差，优于仅使用传感器或传统引导方式。该框架仅需配对的NO和参考数据，且不依赖具体问题结构，具有良好的通用性和可验证性。

item.title: Balti语音语料库及ASR模型研究
item.content:encoded:
Balti语音语料库用于提升自动语音识别性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了BaltiVoice，一个包含16.8小时读音数据的Balti语言语音语料库，该语言属于藏语系，此前无公开的ASR资源。语料库包含10,060个经过验证的发音，源自Mozilla Common Voice录音。研究者在该语料库上对OpenAI Whisper-small模型进行了微调，将词错误率（WER）从初始的182.18%降至30.07%。相关数据集、微调模型和实时转录演示已发布在HuggingFace上。

item.title: 基于结构与语义的视觉令牌剪枝方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出STS框架提升视觉令牌多样性与任务对齐性。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言模型在推理过程中计算开销过大的问题，提出一种名为Structure-to-Semantics (STS) 的两阶段视觉令牌剪枝方法。该方法通过两个阶段实现更高效的令牌选择：第一阶段采用基于排斥的采样机制以最大化空间和结构多样性；第二阶段利用指令感知的跨注意力机制过滤无关令牌。实验表明，STS有效减少了基于注意力的选择带来的冗余，提升了保留视觉令牌的结构多样性和细粒度任务对齐性。

item.title: 基于频率感知的遥感变化检测框架FAF-CD
item.content:encoded:
FAF-CD提升遥感变化检测性能并降低计算成本。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为FAF-CD的频率感知混合框架，用于处理真实世界中不完美异构观测下的遥感变化检测任务。该框架采用DINOv3预训练的ConvNeXt编码器和线性复杂度的VMamba解码器，并引入一个感知校正的三分支融合模块，结合可变形空间对齐与傅里叶和哈尔小波比较，通过自适应门控聚合多尺度互补信息。在BRIGHT验证数据集上，FAF-CD在干净和扰动情况下的tc-mIoU/tc-mAP指标优于NeXt2Former-CD。此外，FAF-CD在二值光学变化检测任务中表现优异，在LEVIR-CD和WHU-CD数据集上分别取得0.924和0.955的cF1值，且在伪变化对齐压力测试下取得最佳平均扰动cIoU/cF1。相比NeXt2Former-CD，FAF-CD降低了约24 GFLOPs的计算成本，同时保持或提升了检测精度。

item.title: Forward-Forward算法在回归任务中的扩展与应用
item.content:encoded:
FFR算法首次将FF扩展至回归任务，性能优于现有方法。



摘要： 本文提出FFR（Forward-Forward for Regression），这是首个将Forward-Forward（FF）算法扩展至真实世界回归任务的框架。FF原本用于分类任务，但回归任务面临目标空间连续、缺乏自然对比对等挑战。FFR通过引入序数竞争性得分函数、分层梯形结构和层次预测与不确定性估计三个创新点，实现了高效的回归学习。实验表明，FFR在五个真实数据集上平均恢复了98.6%的BP精度，同时显著降低了训练内存消耗和时间成本，优于所有无BP方法。

item.title: 基于树状自博弈的代码生成安全提升方法
item.content:encoded:
TSP框架提升代码生成安全性，显著优化漏洞检测效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为树状自博弈（Tree-like Self-Play, TSP）的新框架，用于提升大语言模型在代码生成中的安全性。与传统对齐技术不同，TSP将代码生成视为细粒度的决策过程，通过构建决策树探索安全路径和潜在漏洞路径，从而在关键决策点实现自我修正。实验表明，TSP在Python安全基准测试中将CodeLlama-7B的通过率提升至75.8%，优于SFT和未结构化自博弈基线。此外，TSP还表现出强大的跨语言泛化能力，有效减少未见类别中的漏洞，并成功将C/C++的安全部分迁移至Python、Go和JavaScript等语言，证明其能够内化抽象的安全逻辑而非简单记忆补丁。

item.title: OmniDreams：基于扩散模型的实时驾驶环境生成系统
item.content:encoded:
OmniDreams实现高保真驾驶场景实时生成，提升自动驾驶评估能力。



摘要： 随着自动驾驶技术的发展，长尾场景下的安全评估仍是关键挑战。传统神经模拟器受限于初始数据，难以泛化到动态或新颖场景。为此，研究提出OmniDreams，一个基于Cosmos扩散模型的生成式世界模型，通过中后训练在21000小时驾驶场景上进行优化，能够实时生成动作条件视频，捕捉极端天气和不可预测的动态行为等复杂现象。OmniDreams通过过去帧、当前状态和即时驾驶动作进行自回归生成，作为闭环系统中的响应环境，为自动驾驶策略提供高效评估方案。实验表明，基于OmniDreams训练的世界-动作模型（WAM）在NuRec数据集上表现优异，参数量仅为Alpamayo 1.5的五分之一。
